steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank follows ‘functions’ and precedes a ‘whereas’ clause that explains the mechanism behind how cells develop different functions. The question asks which punctuation correctly joins the main clause to that explanatory dependent clause.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'functions,': A comma alone is too weak to signal that what follows is a full explanation of the preceding statement. ✗
  • Choice B — 'functions': No punctuation at all leaves no signal of the explanatory relationship between the two clauses. ✗
  • Choice C — 'functions and,': Adding ‘and’ with a comma is illogical; ‘and’ coordinates, but the ‘whereas’ clause contrasts and explains rather than simply adding information. ✗
  • Choice D — 'functions:': A colon introduces an elaboration or explanation of the preceding independent clause. The ‘whereas’ clause explains how cells with identical DNA develop different functions — exactly the explanatory role a colon signals. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
